sections.paragraph:
ἐπιγινώσϰω [ἐπί, γινώσκω] the rendering ‘know’ fits all passages, but contextual nuances suggest a variety of resources in Engl.

—1. of familiarity with someth. or someone through observation/experience or receipt of information, know Mt 7:16; Mk 6:54; Ac 27:39; 1 Ti 4:3.

—2. of awareness or recognition based on previous knowledge know, recognize Lk 24:16, 31; Ac 3:10; 4:13; 12:14; 2 Cor 1:13f.

—3. w. ἐπι-in ascensive rolereally know, know well Mt 11:27; Lk 1:4; Ro 1:32; 1 Cor 13:12; 2 Cor 6:9; Col 1:6; 1 Ti 4:3; cp. 1 Cor 16:18 (accord special recognition to).

—4. w. focus on acquisition of knowledge find out Mk 5:30; 6:33; Lk 7:37; Ac 9:30; 22:29; 28:1; make acquaintance of, learn to know 2 Pt 2:21.
